channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump calls on Biden to stop selling unused portions of the border wall
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has called on the Biden administration to stop selling off unused portions of border wall that were purchased but not installed during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Jill Colvin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Paris Hilton heading to Capitol with 'urgent' plea to pass youth welfare program reform
item.description: Paris Hilton is heading back to the Capitol with an urgent plea, calling on "every member of the House" to pass a bill focused on youth congregate care facilities that she says would "create real, meaningful change" before the legislative session ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Hezbollah chief says group lost critical arms supply route from Iran with Syrian ouster of Assad
item.description: Hezbollah lost its most important supply route from Iran through Syria with the fall of dictator Bashar al-Assad, the group’s chief admitted Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court declines appeal from Peter Navarro; Trump White House aide to return after prison term
item.description: The Supreme Court has turned back an appeal from Peter Navarro, the former Trump White House official who is set to return in his second term after serving prison time on contempt of Congress charges.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home to announce that the company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Majority of small businesses anticipate revenue spikes under Trump's 1st year in office: data
item.description: The Q4 MetLife and U.S. Chamber of Commerce Small Business Index report found that small business owners, at 72%, anticipate their revenue to increase next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Satanic Temple's nativity display in New Hampshire destroyed, Dem rep calls for new display
item.description: A Satanic Temple nativity display near the New Hampshire Capitol was destroyed after being vandalized. Now, Democratic state Rep. Ellen Read wants it to get a new display.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mitt Romney names who he thinks will be the GOP's 2028 presidential nominee
item.description: Sen. Mitt Romney predicts that Vice President-elect JD Vance will become the Republican Party's 2028 presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr set to face abortion, vaccine scrutiny in sit-downs with senators on Capitol Hill
item.description: Trump HHS Secretary nominee Robert F. Kennedy Jr. will face questions on several controversial topics from senators when he makes the rounds on Capitol Hill this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: When Donald Trump takes office as president for a second time, many journalists anticipate a hostile climate toward them and their work.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: More than 120 House Democrats send letter to Biden urging him to ratify Equal Rights Amendment
item.description: “We must continue our efforts to fully affirm and recognize the equality of rights for all people, regardless of sex, as part of our Constitution, a vital effort that has never been more urgent,” Congress members penned.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Bernie Sanders believes Hunter Biden pardon sets a 'dangerous' precedent
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders spoke on NBC's "Meet the Press" on Sunday about the Hunter Biden pardon and one way he would work with President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats recalibrate their resistance to Trump
item.description: Democrats are not planning an all-hands resistance to President-elect Trump. At least not in 2016 style, when lawmakers, activists, volunteers and millions of angry voters mounted a party-wide effort to curb his newfound influence in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump appoints longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as special missions envoy
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday that he's picked longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as an envoy for special missions, tasking him with helping the incoming administration deal with some of the toughest foreign policy challenges.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Richard Grenell as presidential envoy for special missions, Edward S. Walsh as Ireland ambassador
item.description: Trump named Richard Grenell, former acting director of national intelligence, as an envoy for special missions and Edward S. Walsh as his pick to serve as ambassador to Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces more nominations, including Devin Nunes, Troy Edgar and Bill White
item.description: Trump nominated Truth Social CEO Devin Nunes as chairman of his Intelligence Advisory Board, Troy Edgar as deputy secretary of Homeland Security and Bill White as ambassador to Belgium.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominees should ‘steer clear’ of undermining polio vaccine, McConnell says
item.description: Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, a childhood polio survivor, said any of President-elect Donald Trump’s picks requiring Senate confirmation should “steer clear” of efforts to discredit the polio vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden urges Congress to 'do something' for gun reform on Sandy Hook anniversary
item.description: Biden in a press release touted his administration's efforts to combat gun violence but argued that there was more to be done — especially as his presidency enters the final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi had hip replacement surgery Saturday at a U.S. military hospital in Germany after falling while at an event in Luxembourg with other members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU: Officials should enact law 'firewall' ahead of Trump 2nd term
item.description: ACLU leaders told press Wednesday that state and city governments should prepare for the implementation of ideas from Project 2025.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin: Biden 'went completely insane on me' over American Rescue Plan opposition
item.description: “I was going to kill the whole bill. Then the president went completely insane on me,” Manchin said in an interview with news outlet Semafor that was published on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants to turn the clock on daylight saving time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump wants to turn the lights out on daylight saving time. In a post on his social media site Friday, Trump said his party would try to end the practice when he returns to office.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden tells military children at Marine Corps Reserve toy drive to remember that they are loved
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Less than 10 minutes was all it took for a large pile of toys donated by the White House staff to disappear as Jill Biden and children from military families sorted them into boxes as part of the annual Marine Corps Reserve Toys for Tots campaign for the less fortunate.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raskin demands FBI vetting of Trump nominees, details on Epshteyn
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) on Friday made a series of demands of the Trump transition team, asking for assurances President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees will undergo FBI vetting while also requesting details about aide Boris Epshteyn’s alleged profiting from promoting those seeking new posts.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These are the Trump appointees who have faced legal troubles
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several people to his next administration who have faced legal troubles — including Trump himself, who was convicted in a New York hush money case during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi hospitalized in Luxembourg after sustaining injury from fall on stairs
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) was hospitalized for evaluation in Luxembourg after falling on a set of stairs and sustaining an injury during a trip with a congressional delegation.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis City Council overrides mayor’s veto of 2025 budget
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council enacted its 2025 budget on Thursday, after overturning a veto by Mayor Jacob Frey.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News
